info.title: Tableau REST API
info.description:
The Tableau REST API enables programmatic access to Tableau Server and Tableau Cloud resources. It provides comprehensive management of workbooks, data sources, views, users, groups, projects, permissions, schedules, subscriptions, favorites, flows, and more.

Capabilities include:
- Authentication via Personal Access Tokens (PAT), username/password, Connected Apps (JWT), or Unified Access Tokens
- Workbook and view management - publish, download, update, refresh, and delete workbooks
- Data source management - publish, update connections, refresh extracts, download
- User and group administration - create, update, remove users and manage group membership
- Project organization - create hierarchical project structures for content management
- Granular permissions - assign and manage permissions at project, workbook, data source, and view levels
- Scheduling - create and manage extract refresh and subscription schedules
- Favorites and subscriptions - manage user content preferences and email subscriptions
- Flow management - publish, run, update, and manage Prep flows
- Server and site administration - query server info, manage sites and settings

servers.description: Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud instance

server.description: Your Tableau Server hostname or Tableau Cloud pod URL (e.g., 10ax.online.tableau.com, us-east-1.online.tableau.com, or your-server.example.com)

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Sign in and sign out of Tableau Server or Cloud to obtain and manage authentication tokens

tags.name: Server
tags.description: Retrieve server-level information including version, build, and REST API version

tags.name: Sites
tags.description: Manage Tableau sites including creation, updates, queries, and deletion

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Manage projects used to organize and secure content on a site

tags.name: Workbooks
tags.description: Publish, download, update, refresh, and manage workbooks and their connections

tags.name: Views
tags.description: Query views, retrieve images, PDFs, data, and manage view recommendations

tags.name: Data Sources
tags.description: Publish, update, refresh, download, and manage data sources and their connections

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Manage site users including adding, updating, removing, and querying user information

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: Manage groups and group membership for organizing users

tags.name: Permissions
tags.description: Query, add, and delete granular permissions on projects, workbooks, data sources, views, and flows

tags.name: Schedules
tags.description: Manage server schedules for extract refreshes and subscriptions (Tableau Server only)

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Manage email subscriptions to views and workbooks

tags.name: Favorites
tags.description: Manage user favorites for quick access to workbooks, views, data sources, projects, and flows

tags.name: Flows
tags.description: Publish, run, update, and manage Tableau Prep flows

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Monitor and manage asynchronous jobs such as extract refreshes and flow runs

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Manage extract refresh tasks and subscription tasks

post.description: Signs in to Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud and returns an authentication token. The token must be included in the X-Tableau-Auth header of all subsequent requests. Supports authentication via username/password or Personal Access Token (PAT).

usernamePassword.summary: Sign in with username and password

personalAccessToken.summary: Sign in with Personal Access Token

post.description: Signs out of Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud and invalidates the current authentication token.
Switches the authenticated user's context to a different site, returning a new authentication token for the target site.

contentUrl.description: The URL namespace of the target site

get.description: Returns information about the Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud instance, including product version, build number, and the REST API version supported.
Returns a list of sites on the server that the authenticated user has access to.

post.description: Creates a new site on the server. Only server administrators can create sites.

get.description: Returns information about the specified site.

parameters.name: includeUsage
parameters.description: If true, includes storage usage information for the site

put.description: Updates the specified site. Only server or site administrators can update sites.

delete.description: Deletes the specified site. Only server administrators can delete sites.

get.description: Returns a list of projects on the specified site. Projects organize workbooks, data sources, and other content.

post.description: Creates a new project on the specified site. Projects can be nested by specifying a parentProjectId.

put.description: Updates the name, description, or parent project of the specified project.

delete.description: Deletes the specified project. Deleting a project also deletes all content (workbooks, data sources) in the project.

get.description: Returns the workbooks on the specified site that the authenticated user has read access to.

post.description: Publishes a workbook to the specified site. The workbook can be published as a .twb or .twbx file. For files larger than 64 MB, use multipart upload.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: If true, overwrites an existing workbook with the same name

parameters.name: asJob
parameters.description: If true, the publish operation runs as an asynchronous job

parameters.name: skipConnectionCheck
parameters.description: If true, skips connection validation during publish

get.description: Returns information about the specified workbook, including its views, tags, and project.

put.description: Updates the name, project, owner, or show-tabs setting of the specified workbook.

delete.description: Deletes the specified workbook from the site.

get.description: Downloads the specified workbook as a .twb or .twbx file.

parameters.name: extractValue
parameters.description: If true, includes the extract in the download (for .twbx files)

get.description: Returns the data connections for the specified workbook.

put.description: Updates the server address, port, username, or password for the specified workbook connection.

post.description: Refreshes the extracts in the specified workbook. This is an asynchronous operation that creates a job.

put.description: Adds one or more tags to the specified workbook.

delete.description: Deletes the specified tag from the workbook.

parameters.name: tag-name
parameters.description: The name of the tag to delete

get.description: Returns the workbooks that the specified user owns or has read access to.
Returns all views on the specified site that the authenticated user has access to.

parameters.name: includeUsageStatistics
parameters.description: If true, includes usage statistics for each view

get.description: Returns information about the specified view.
Returns a PNG image of the specified view. You can specify filter values and resolution.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: Image resolution (standard or high)

parameters.name: maxAge
parameters.description: Maximum age of the cached image in minutes

parameters.name: vf_<filter_name>
parameters.description: View filter values. Replace <filter_name> with the actual filter field name.

get.description: Returns a PDF rendering of the specified view.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Page type for the PDF

parameters.name: orientation
parameters.description: Page orientation for the PDF

get.description: Returns the underlying data of the specified view in CSV format.
Returns a crosstab (Excel) rendering of the data in the specified view.
Returns the views in the specified workbook.

parameters.name: includeUsageStatistics
parameters.description: If true, includes usage statistics for each view

get.description: Returns the data sources on the specified site that the authenticated user has access to.

post.description: Publishes a data source to the specified site. The data source file can be a .tds, .tdsx, or .hyper file. For files larger than 64 MB, use multipart upload.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: If true, overwrites an existing data source with the same name

parameters.name: append
parameters.description: If true, appends data to an existing data source

parameters.name: asJob
parameters.description: If true, the publish operation runs as an asynchronous job

get.description: Returns information about the specified data source.

put.description: Updates the name, project, owner, or certification status of the specified data source.

delete.description: Deletes the specified data source from the site.

get.description: Downloads the specified data source as a .tds or .tdsx file.

parameters.name: includeExtract
parameters.description: If false, excludes the extract from the download

get.description: Returns the data connections for the specified data source.

put.description: Updates the server address, port, username, or password for the specified data source connection.

post.description: Refreshes the extract for the specified data source. This is an asynchronous operation that creates a job.

put.description: Adds one or more tags to the specified data source.

delete.description: Deletes the specified tag from the data source.

parameters.name: tag-name
parameters.description: The name of the tag to delete

get.description: Returns the users on the specified site.

post.description: Adds a user to the specified site. The user must already exist on the server (for Tableau Server) or have an identity in the identity pool (for Tableau Cloud).

get.description: Returns information about the specified user.

put.description: Updates the specified user's name, email, password, or site role.

delete.description: Removes the specified user from the site. Content owned by the user must be reassigned before removal.

parameters.name: mapAssetsTo
parameters.description: The user ID to reassign the removed user's content to

get.description: Returns the groups on the specified site.

post.description: Creates a new group on the specified site.

put.description: Updates the name or minimum site role of the specified group.

delete.description: Deletes the specified group from the site.

get.description: Returns the users in the specified group.

post.description: Adds the specified user to the group.

id.description: The ID of the user to add

delete.description: Removes the specified user from the group.

get.description: Returns the permissions for the specified project.

put.description: Adds permissions for a user or group to the specified project.

delete.description: Deletes a specific permission capability for a user on the specified project.
Deletes a specific permission capability for a group on the specified project.

get.description: Returns the permissions for the specified workbook.

put.description: Adds permissions for a user or group to the specified workbook.

get.description: Returns the permissions for the specified data source.

put.description: Adds permissions for a user or group to the specified data source.

get.summary: Query Default Workbook Permissions for Project
get.description: Returns the default permissions for workbooks in the specified project.

put.summary: Add Default Workbook Permissions for Project
put.description: Sets default permissions for new workbooks created in the specified project.

get.summary: Query Default Data Source Permissions for Project
get.description: Returns the default permissions for data sources in the specified project.

put.summary: Add Default Data Source Permissions for Project
put.description: Sets default permissions for new data sources created in the specified project.

get.description: Returns all schedules on the server. Tableau Server only.

post.description: Creates a new schedule for extract refreshes or subscriptions. Tableau Server only.

put.description: Updates the specified schedule. Tableau Server only.

delete.description: Deletes the specified schedule. Tableau Server only.

get.description: Returns the subscriptions on the specified site.

post.description: Creates a new subscription for the specified view or workbook.

get.description: Returns information about the specified subscription.

put.description: Updates the subject, schedule, or suspend status of the specified subscription.

get.description: Returns the favorites for the specified user, including workbooks, views, data sources, projects, and flows.

put.description: Adds a workbook, view, data source, project, or flow to the specified user's favorites.

delete.description: Removes the specified workbook from the user's favorites.
Removes the specified view from the user's favorites.
Removes the specified data source from the user's favorites.
Removes the specified project from the user's favorites.

get.description: Returns the flows on the specified site that the authenticated user has access to.

post.description: Publishes a Tableau Prep flow to the specified site.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: If true, overwrites an existing flow with the same name

get.description: Returns information about the specified flow.

put.description: Updates the name, project, or owner of the specified flow.

delete.description: Deletes the specified flow from the site.

get.description: Returns the data connections for the specified flow.

post.description: Runs the specified flow immediately. Returns a job that can be used to track the run status.

get.description: Downloads the specified flow as a .tfl or .tflx file.
Returns the permissions for the specified flow.

put.description: Adds permissions for a user or group to the specified flow.

get.description: Returns the asynchronous jobs on the specified site. Jobs are created for extract refreshes, workbook/data source publishing, and flow runs.
Returns status information about the specified job.

put.description: Cancels the specified job if it is still in progress.

get.description: Returns the extract refresh tasks on the specified site.
Returns information about the specified extract refresh task.

delete.description: Deletes the specified extract refresh task.

post.description: Runs the specified extract refresh task immediately.

tableauAuth.name: X-Tableau-Auth
tableauAuth.description: Tableau authentication token obtained from the Sign In endpoint (POST /auth/signin). This token must be included in all subsequent API requests. Tokens are short-lived and expire after a period of inactivity. To obtain a token, sign in with either username/password or a Personal Access Token (PAT).

personalAccessToken.name: X-Tableau-Auth
personalAccessToken.description: Authentication using a Personal Access Token (PAT). PATs are long-lived tokens created in Tableau Server or Cloud user settings. To authenticate, include the PAT name and secret in the Sign In request body, then use the returned session token in the X-Tableau-Auth header. PATs are recommended for automated scripts and CI/CD pipelines. They expire after 15 consecutive days of non-use and can be individually revoked without affecting the user account.

connectedAppJwt.description: JWT-based authentication for Tableau Connected Apps. Connected Apps enable trusted authentication between Tableau and external applications without requiring user credentials. Create a Connected App in Tableau Server or Cloud settings to obtain a client ID and generate a secret. Use these to create a JWT with scopes like tableau:views:embed, tableau:content:read, etc. The JWT is passed directly as a Bearer token. Supported scopes include: tableau:content:read, tableau:workbooks:create, tableau:datasources:create, tableau:tasks:run, tableau:users:create, tableau:groups:create, tableau:projects:create, tableau:workbooks:download, tableau:datasources:download.

siteIdPath.name: site-id
siteIdPath.description: The LUID of the site. Obtained from the Sign In response or Query Sites.

datasourceIdPath.name: datasource-id
datasourceIdPath.description: The LUID of the data source

capabilityNamePath.name: capability-name
capabilityNamePath.description: The name of the capability (e.g., Read, Write, ChangePermissions, Delete, Filter, ViewComments, AddComment, ExportData, ViewUnderlyingData, ExportImage, ShareView, WebAuthoring, RunExplainData, CreateRefreshMetrics)

capabilityModePath.name: capability-mode
capabilityModePath.description: The mode of the capability: Allow or Deny

pageSize.name: pageSize
pageSize.description: The number of items to return per page (max 1000, default 100)

filter.name: filter
filter.description: Filter expression to limit results. Format: field:operator:value. Multiple filters joined with commas. Operators: eq, in, gt, gte, lt, lte, has. Example: name:eq:MyWorkbook,ownerName:eq:admin

sort.name: sort
sort.description: Sort expression. Format: field:direction. Multiple sorts joined with commas. Direction: asc or desc. Example: name:asc,createdAt:desc

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include in the response. Use _default_ for default fields, or specify individual field names.

BadRequest.description: 400 - Bad request. The request body is malformed, missing required fields, or contains invalid values.

Unauthorized.description: 401 - Unauthorized. The authentication token is missing, invalid, or expired. Sign in again to obtain a new token.

Forbidden.description: 403 - Forbidden. The authenticated user does not have the required permissions for this operation.

NotFound.description: 404 - Not found. The specified resource does not exist or the user does not have access to it.

Conflict.description: 409 - Conflict. A resource with the same name or identifier already exists.

summary.description: A short summary of the error

personalAccessTokenName.description: Name of the Personal Access Token (use with personalAccessTokenSecret)

personalAccessTokenSecret.description: Secret value of the Personal Access Token

contentUrl.description: The URL namespace of the site to sign in to. Use empty string for the default site.

token.description: The authentication token to use in subsequent requests via the X-Tableau-Auth header

estimatedTimeToExpiration.description: Estimated time until the token expires

contentUrl.description: The URL namespace of the site

id.description: The LUID of the authenticated user

restApiVersion.description: REST API version supported by this server

contentUrl.description: URL namespace of the site (used in URLs)

userQuota.description: Maximum number of users for the site

contentPermissions.description: How permissions are managed for content in this project

parentProjectId.description: LUID of the parent project (null for top-level projects)

size.description: Size of the workbook in bytes

type.description: The type of the data source (e.g., live, extract)
Connection type (e.g., sqlserver, postgres, mysql, snowflake, bigquery)

embed.description: If true, embeds credentials in the data source

oAuth.description: If true, uses OAuth for the connection

siteRole.description: The user's role on the site

name.description: Username of the user to add
Capability name (e.g., Read, Write, ChangePermissions, Delete, Filter, ViewComments, AddComment, ExportData, ViewUnderlyingData, ExportImage, ShareView, WebAuthoring)

type.description: Type of job (e.g., RefreshExtract, PublishWorkbook, RunFlow)
